74392.subject: Moonshots
74392.body:
Editor's Digest 

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: Financial Times


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: Financial Times

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/moonshots-financial-times-jap0e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BbWDUoUo2Sr%2BApL0USWj38w%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=35MTOMvobL-Yg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mq9vshj7~i3-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mq9vshj7-i3
  
On Friday Elon MuskвАЩs SpaceX could make the biggest stock market debut in history. The AI-to-satellite conglomerate wants to raise $86bn at a $1.78tn valuation, meaning it would trade at 92 times its revenue last year вАФ a multiple that far outstrips that of other Big Tech companies. Much of SpaceXвАЩs lofty price tag is based on a series of ambitious вАЬmoonshotвАЭ goals: to reach Mars with reusable rockets, put data centres into orbit and catch up with rival AI labs Anthropic, Google and OpenAI. As this visual story explains, ordinary investors may have no choice but to come along for the ride. My choices this week Our reporters got two big, Vladimir Putin-related scoops this week. The first reveals how his security services reacted to IsraelвАЩs assassination of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The second uncovers how former Chelsea owner Roman Abramovich was involved in UkraineвАЩs failed attempt to hold direct talks with the Russian president. In another FT exclusive, we reveal that the owners of British pharmacy chain Boots are in talks over a $10bn sale. Find out why the deal may come as a disappointment to bankers in the City of London. IntesaвАЩs вВђ30.6bn takeover bid for Monte dei Paschi di Siena is the latest twist in a consolidation saga that has engulfed Italian banking since the government returned MPS to private hands in 2024. Go inside the battle to break up the worldвАЩs oldest bank. Who will win the World Cup? ItвАЩs an unanswerable question. But these predictive patterns might just provide a clue. Donald Trump is keeping one of the worldвАЩs most dangerous flashpoints in suspense. As the threat from China rises, will the US president abandon Taiwan? (Free to read) The industrial revolution keeps cropping up in arguments over the potential impact of artificial intelligence on workers. But as FT columnist Sarah OвАЩConnor argues, if we want to understand how our predecessors navigated profound change, we are looking at the wrong evidence. (Free to read) Thanks for reading, Roula Khalaf, FT editor Recommended newsletters for you White House Watch What TrumpвАЩs second term means for Washington, business and the world. Sign up here Sort Your Financial Life Out Learn how to make smarter money decisions and supercharge your personal finances with Claer Barrett. Sign up here
–Я—А–Њ–і–Њ–≤–ґ–Є—В–Є —З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/moonshots-financial-times-jap0e?lipi=ur

74391.subject: The global fragmentation of fun
74391.body:

 The Economist

June 11th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist This Week

 Highlights from the latest issue

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApOGRzaXy53ZOrCzd_kT5gMjV045-bb2_mXYQDcmygbrCjPdBrtLOAh5ZnMxjL4D85EBbTPs_Hfhno2QyCvkzLtGukl27RRgV3sXQAD0dh1BgA 



https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApOGRzaY33UyQRsQ2NHYouNUL-hFqictADin1NsgDws17S_jPmRouIyzAzmtjcp0-03QOFpAoJ20UI5kllkaZiper2IHGg3Nadzz2KPq 



Robert Guest

Deputy editor



 In the days when Amazon was just an online bookseller, I gave a speech celebrating how digital technology was broadening people’s horizons. From the comfort of your home, I gushed to an audience of young Brits and Germans, you can order novels from Brazil, manga from Japan, astrophysics textbooks from America—and they will arrive in a matter of days! (I was then upstaged by a not-yet-famous Michael Gove performing a rap about the European Union, but that is another story.) 


 It turns out that I was wrong. As our 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiO

74389.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: Canton's $355 million raise
74389.body:
Citigroup to offer tokenized shares of private companies for wealthy and institutional clients | South Korean police book Bithumb CEO as bribery suspect in lawmaker hiring probe | BlackRock files new amendment for yield-generating bitcoin ETF; launch expected soon | Bernstein calls FIFA World Cup a 'watershed moment' for prediction markets, projects $5-10B consumer volume surge |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Thursday! *Bitfinex* analysts said ( https://blog.bitfinex.com/bitfinex-alpha/the-btc-air-gap-closes/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *bitcoin's* recent sell-off has largely flushed excess leverage from the market, shifting focus to ETF demand and next week's *FOMC* meeting as the key drivers of the next move.

In today's newsletter, *Canton* developer *Digital Asset* raises $355 million, *Citigroup* plans to offer tokenized shares of private companies, *South Korean* police book *Bithumb's* CEO as a suspect in a bribery investigation, and more.

Meanwhile, on the ground at *BTC Prague* , *Strike* and *Twenty One* CEO *Jack Mallers* said ( https://www.theblock.co/post/404453/sell-what-you-can-not-what-you-want-jack-mallers-bitcoin-pricing-global-liquidity-crisis?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *bitcoin* is pricing in a global liquidity crisis: "you sell what you can, not what you want."

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯТ∞ a16z crypto leads $355 million raise for Canton developer Digital Asset*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/404386/a16z-crypto-leads-355-million-raise-for-canton-developer-digital-asset?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Digital Asset* , the company developing the *Canton Network* , raised $355 million in a funding round led by *a16z crypto* , with participation from major TradFi and crypto firms.

* CEO *Yuval Rooz* said the fresh capital will help Digital Asset accelerate strategic partnerships, pursue acquisitions, and support ecosystem growth.
* Many of the new investors received equity rather than token allocations and are expected to become users of the Canton Network.
* Canton claims it has supported the issuance of $6 trillion in tokenized assets and counts f

74388.subject: рЯЧЮпЄП Cycle Low, Strategy's Reversal and Institutions Buying the Dip at $65K
74388.body:
Bitcoin fell to $59,100. Here is what the data says about who
stepped in.    


Kraken ( " target= )  -->  Kraken ( " target= )




**********************************
Compass
**********************************

Delivering Insights & News

See latest prices (
https://link.kraken.com/f/a/4RXP7InNjjHLsvW6rGg9Zg~~/AAQRxRA~/p_DNBZmRwT6t2qpltwqBwL8GuOXMhaIkl4ROZiUpgMrcxS1koU3nOl-5d8FAUWGpNEN95Rbx_H24QKmD65ntm5trpkjH3xGCyg0XqMWIqKvqQMqdlfhxkVWp57Wn3MR-MhUfYUfzswsABn93KJ8Jlg~~
)     Kraken              

*****************************************************************
BTC Below $60K, a Blowout Jobs Report and StrategyвАЩs Rapid
Reversal
*****************************************************************

June 5 вАУ 11, 2026

Bitcoin touched a cycle low of $59,100 on Friday after May
nonfarm payrolls came in at 172,000, more than double the 85,000
consensus forecast, sending the Nasdaq down 4.2 percent in its
worst session since April 2025 and reviving fears of a Federal
Reserve rate hike. The asset has partially recovered to
approximately $63,400 at the time of writing, a weekly range of
roughly $5,100 and a decline of around 10 percent from last
ThursdayвАЩs open. The Fear and Greed Index hit eight on Saturday,
its lowest reading since 2022.

Against that backdrop, Strategy reversed course within one
week, purchasing 1,550 BTC for $101 million just days after its
first disclosed sale in years, while Bernstein analysts argued in
a Monday note that the current holder base is structurally more
stable than any prior retail-driven cycle. WednesdayвАЩs May CPI
print, forecast at 4.2 percent, and the June 16 to 17 FOMC
meeting will now determine whether the macro pressure holding
Bitcoin below $65,000 eases or intensifies.





---------------
Market Overview
---------------

A Jobs Report That Shocked Markets Sent Bitcoin to Its Lowest
Level Since October 2024 (
https://link.kraken.com/f/a/MlfkzLB17YvV1Bec9_kxjA~~/AAQRxRA~/8GYwtViWcYcXwharcKQ-FYQcYqp9kXGotDwZGNvPGExOvgBi6PaW4Y574C6l1Rm8iN7tNjP-Evxq4-z52K-WbmZ290LNpVTvMSmrTeEEo7jjMOVi-DOjjs2PH4WS-MvU
)

)  Source: Coindesk 
( https://link.kraken.com/f/a/SqrnzXuYsYlspwFmZfm4WQ~~/AAQRxRA~/yVs1_OhwVb9oojTD_Wrmi9A_5wR-m2AElclE9u-qajN4vEi2KHxn1F6FZbqCACZqclBTv8eWR2LZck1DHTDUSFNM0uL2QC8JYP8pDOwSpRcq8ClhHo1PIy5Z4VP99v4Xek_XS6r9WJ3M0rBOwMHNkc_Yoxd4vK5XW4baLad9_pU~ ). Data

74383.subject: Ukrainian strikes are inflicting pain deep inside Russia
74383.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApIvEMBklZ3fryhtY6_LelJoMNJ-_RGuYUFqDuVQeIbvQihLo2aIomIbqQ4J1jbUdOuJjZ9GJrU1SPAMgEW152TjRxNjcYKt8zQF6NjEw6WfJA 

June 11th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApIvEMBlpC1kEJbwvGSj0c76h_xOCxuXTyRQB3GwGqINI2ybTxOlaTExx-T8Bn8EM-KsXJVG_98AKWwkUAUQUwrhqovf-H7d6_6zUc92wLfgWQ 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApIvEMBmpxKBfsHJ9gIcp2dOAZYSmUINkUfAX8Vu3PDkYELPAq2TVc684zEiWJfuepwkIrZe1swWO5GHQL2wqWNGwZFj2grLJFvMT6SeaNtGdQ 



 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



Smoke billowing over St Petersburg, as it did last week after a Ukrainian drone strike, brings Vladimir Putin’s war close to home for Russians. But such images fail to capture the scale of these attacks. Our interactive team has modelled 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApIvEMBnSBzz6X7WRYKE9PJf3EvWNwnbc527eA5XqA3f_5fhJk2W-VqD9PY8A-rqkhBILLB0525wkGCYV2_R3eYljWcFbMk3knk-Jl4i 
thousands of strikes inside Russia  to gauge their power.


If Elon Musk’s big IPO goes to plan, he may soon be the world’s first dollar trillionaire. Why would anyone want that? Better to be a 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDR9AAcAAAAAApIvEMBow8z1Z9Ca5nYn_wNIccrpc1GKfI3s9yZ4gilBKScpt-eezHAh
